message:
fix(fcp-conformance): retire shadow cross-zone assertion (br-1vcqs)

Closes flywheel_connectors-1vcqs (P2/MEDIUM/MOR).

MOR review found the advertised cross-zone conformance lane was a
self-contained string-typed toy model. connector_verification_e2e.rs
only asserted fcp_conformance::interop::cross_zone::run_tests()
passed, and the interop module at
crates/fcp-conformance/src/interop/cross_zone.rs:366-537 defined its
OWN local CrossZoneRequest / ApprovalToken / ReasonCode /
evaluate_cross_zone_access implementation. Those tests could stay
green indefinitely while real fcp_core / fcp_host cross-zone
enforcement regressed.

Fix (option b per the reviewer's suggested mitigation): remove the
shadow assertion from connector_verification_e2e.rs so the
conformance lane no longer broadcasts false coverage, and retain the
interop module in-tree as living documentation of the
decision-receipt reason-code surface a real suite should cover.

Changes:
- connector_verification_e2e.rs: deleted `interop_cross_zone_tests`;
  replaced with a comment block explaining why the assertion was
  retired and pointing at the follow-up bead.
- interop/cross_zone.rs: module docstring now opens with a bold
  "SHADOW MODEL — NOT A REAL CONFORMANCE CHECK" warning, documents
  why the types don't bind to fcp_core / fcp_host, and instructs
  future callers NOT to re-wire the module into the conformance
  binary without delegating to real APIs.

Filed follow-up bead flywheel_connectors-crr6l for building real
host-backed cross-zone conformance coverage that drives
PolicyEngine::evaluate_invoke with genuine ApprovalTokens and asserts
the production DecisionReasonCode surface
(TaintCrossZoneUnapproved, ApprovalMissingDeclassification,
ApprovalZoneMismatch, ApprovalExpired, ApprovalTokenInvalid, Allow).
The follow-up identifies an upstream blocker: minimal_zone_policy /
minimal_decision_input helpers in fcp-core/src/policy.rs are
#[cfg(test)]-gated and need either a testkit feature-gated pub(crate)
export or local re-fixturing in fcp-conformance.

Verified:
- cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test connector_verification_e2e:
  38/38 pass (assertion removal doesn't drop anything else).
- cargo test -p fcp-conformance: only the pre-existing
  wasi_preopened_filesystem_conformance_blocks_writes_and_escape
  failure remains (confirmed baseline-equivalent via stash + rerun).

Cod_4 / SunnyMoose

files.patch:
@@ -1,7 +1,26 @@
 //! Cross-zone enforcement interop tests.
 //!
-//! Tests for cross-zone access control, `ApprovalToken` verification,
-//! and `DecisionReceipt` reason code stability.
+//! **SHADOW MODEL — NOT A REAL CONFORMANCE CHECK (br-1vcqs).**
+//!
+//! This module defines its own local `CrossZoneRequest`, `ApprovalToken`,
+//! `ReasonCode`, `DecisionReceipt`, and `evaluate_cross_zone_access`
+//! (see the test-support section at the bottom of this file). NONE of
+//! those types bind to `fcp_core::policy::PolicyEngine`,
+//! `fcp_core::provenance::ApprovalToken`, or any `fcp_host` enforcement
+//! path. The suite asserts self-consistent behavior of the shadow model
+//! — it can stay green indefinitely while real cross-zone enforcement
+//! regresses.
+//!
+//! The `connector_verification_e2e.rs::interop_cross_zone_tests`
+//! assertion that called `run_tests()` has been removed to avoid
+//! giving false conformance signal. This module remains in-tree as
+//! living documentation of the decision-receipt reason-code surface
+//! that a real host-backed cross-zone conformance suite should cover,
+//! and as a reference for the follow-up work tracked in beads.
+//!
+//! DO NOT wire this module back into the conformance test binary
+//! without first making the types delegate to real fcp_core /
+//! fcp_host APIs.
 
 use crate::interop::{InteropTestSummary, TestFailure};
